mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical relationship with FEDFUNDS changes over a 3-year rolling window (Rank IC < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by > 0.2 percentage points month-over-month
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns remain positive in more than 55% of instances when UNRATE rises above the 0.2pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in real personal income and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer and investment demand expansion, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns with a Rank IC above 0.04 over any 2-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which translates to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days during periods of stable monetary policy.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth shows zero predictive power for 5-day forward returns when FEDFUNDS remains unchanged, with a t-statistic below 1.5 over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation above a 0.4% monthly threshold forces expectations of tighter monetary policy, compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns are not significantly negative when CPIAUCSL exceeds the 0.4% monthly threshold, with a mean return above 0.1%.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total nonfarm payrolls and falling unemployment rates confirms robust labor market tightening, supporting corporate earnings growth and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The PAYEMS-UNRATE divergence signal yields a negative or zero Rank IC for 60-day forward returns across all tested rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real gross domestic product directly correlates with aggregate corporate revenue expansion, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show no statistically significant correlation with 20-day forward returns over a 4-year rolling window (p-value > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive monthly declines in the industrial production index signal a manufacturing contraction that reduces near-term corporate cash flow expectations, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 5-day returns remain positive in more than 50% of instances following two consecutive monthly INDPRO declines.
